bullets.text: Full-year CR 94.9, beating the 96.0 target despite CR of 99.7 through H1; all three segments earned underwriting profit — Personal Lines 6.2%, Commercial Lines 1.2%, Property 1.1%.
Companywide NPW grew 20% to $61.6 billion (+$10.5B YOY); Personal Lines NPW +24%, Property +18%, Commercial Lines +8%; growth achieved despite intentional new business slowdown mid-year.

Personal auto rate increases of 19% in 2023 (vs. 13% in 2022), with some still earning in; 2022's 13% rate increases continue earning through; product models upgraded in 17 personal auto states, 19 commercial states, 14 property states.

Debt-to-total capital ended at 25.4%; $500M bonds issued in 2023 as capital buffer; $0.75/share annual-variable dividend declared; investment portfolio returned 6.3% (fixed income 5.4%, equity 26.7%); duration raised to 3.0 years.

Progressive reached #2 personal auto carrier in U.S.; Barbie movie co-marketing partnership; Dr. Rick campaign won Cannes Gold Lion; advertising cut 50% in H2 to preserve capital, full year spend down $433M (1.4 pts on expense ratio).

Overall expense ratio down 1.2 pts primarily from lower advertising; Personal Lines NAER up slightly from compensation; Commercial Lines NAER up 0.4 pts from product/systems investment; Property NAER up 1.3 pts from underwriting investments.

BOP in 44 states (70% of CL multiperil market) at year-end; BQX online marketplace with 10 carriers; HomeQuote Explorer available in 47 states; 30% of Commercial Lines policyholders also had Personal Lines policy.

Gallup Exceptional Workplace Award for 3rd consecutive year; Forbes #1 Best Employer for Diversity; 60,000+ employees received new Volunteer Time Off benefit; Name Your Cause charitable participation rose from 10% to nearly 40%.

Personal auto severity trends improving in second half; Florida HB 837 tort reform reserves adequately recognized; prior-year development unfavorable 2.7 pts YTD through Q3 but moderating; commercial loss costs rising across physical damage and bodily injury.

Keys to Progress gifted its 1,000th vehicle to veterans since 2013 inception; monthly employee videos reduced organizational uncertainty during challenging year; internal communications credited as key factor in turnaround execution.
